title: For Immediate Release – Office of State Senator Josh Becker – Friday, May 21, 2021
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: The worldwide concrete and cement industry accounts for almost 8 percent of global emissions.
SB 596 sets a 2045 target date in California for carbon neutral cement and concrete, with a 40% emissions reduction by 2030.
Transportation is the greatest source of greenhouse gas emissions in California.
SB 771 provides a state sales tax break for lower income Californians purchasing electric or hybrid vehicles.
SB 68 aims to lower costs and reduce delays for property owners seeking to upgrade their electric panels.
SB 767 establishes the Digital Education Equity Program to provide education technology and network support to California public school districts.
SB 775 aims to clarify existing law to allow less culpable individuals convicted of attempted murder to petition for modified sentences.
SB 355 seeks to improve access to civil justice by updating eligibility criteria for low-income Californians seeking fee waivers.
SB 503 would implement a uniform statewide standard for signature verification of mail-in ballots.
Challenged signatures disproportionately impact vulnerable communities, including those with limited English proficiency and individuals living with disabilities.
SB 504 ensures that Californians involved with the criminal justice system maintain access to voter registration.
